title: excoecaria insignis
intro: Excoecaria insignis, also known as the Poison Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its shiny leaves and milky sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria insignis include reducing fever, pain relief, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic properties.
